text: That are 10 to the chair of the working group. Oh, Savina, up to the door of Norway. To please be sent a report on this room. Thank you chair and good morning to all delegates. Let me start with the correction and include Palau on the attendance list. This is in WP4. And now I can continue with my reports. You will recall that my opening lines in reporting to you is the following. I am happy to report a product and not a problem. That is the case also today. And I want to thank all participants for the constructive corporations and trust demonstrated in our work. As instructed by the committee, the group considered all documents referred to it on the draft work plan to prepare for the entrance to falls of the IAM on its fair framework. Having incorporated various suggestions, including from UAE on long term measures, Egypt on technology transfer, ICS and on-set and sets rewards, the group finalized the work plan and recommended subject to adoption of the IAM on its fair framework that the committee approved the work plan set out in annex 1 to our report. Mr. Chair, our report also reflects the concerns and reservations by several delegations which considered that it was premature to plan towards implementation of the IMO-NIT-SIL framework. The group also added a cover note clarifying that the work plan was intended as a planning tool providing indicative timelines for the different work plans. The group also considered the proposal made by the UAE to the committee here in cleanery for the cover resolution. After a constructive debate, the overall science made compromises, the group agreed to recommend the inclusion of two paragraphs in the draft resolution on adoption of the 2025 revised. The first paragraph is marked by the following six as follows. You will find that in paragraph 17 of the report that I will read it out. Point 1, after the fourth pre-embranning paragraph on the draft resolution, inserts the following text. Reaffirming also that the IAM on its fair framework is the only global midterm measure for international shipping. In the interest of avoiding a possible patchwork of duplicative state regional lists. This agreement demonstrates the willingness in this group to work together to resolve sensible issues. Finally, again, I want to thank the Secretariat, the IMO-DSD team. We could not have completed our work on terms or efforts and delivered a report to you without their excellent support. As always, I leave the action points to you, sir. Thank you very much. I would like to thank the chair of the group and particularly the group for the report. Thank you for the hard work and spread of compromise. I have read this document before us. Now, before taking the action point, I like it as a floor of Brazil. Thank you very much, chair. I'd like to first frame my condolences to Kenya for the personal form of prime minister. I would have liked to do that yesterday, but I never had the chance. Chair, did this indeed an extraordinary meeting? Yesterday, we have been prevented from speaking for three times over two hours of waiting. Even after trying to behave in a disciplined manner and participate constructively throughout the whole session. But the more worrisome aspect was how evident it became for all to see that the actual meeting is not happening here. The way the session is unfolding, it seems like we have been using time in a not all is fruitful way. And we know how time is being used out of here in bilateral context that involves tools and methods that have nothing to do with the IMO, that should not have anything to do with this organization nor with multilateralism in our view. The methods that should not ever be used amongst our nations. And we hope that this is not replacing negotiations as the normal way for us to roll over decisions. Otherwise, there will be no more decisions to be made. Yesterday, we witnessed something very peculiar. In short, one delegation requested a procedure that in the view of another went against a previous ruling. We could have quickly settled a matter in one of two ways, one by checking the records. And we would have confirmed that you ruled to go along with the suggestion by Brazil. To my understanding, the issue would have been settled then in light of clear absence. Or two, if there was some misunderstanding perhaps the easiest way forward would have been as we and many others requested that the issue was simply clarified. Well, as we seem to be having issues with time, but have had enough time to discuss with other delegations, we would like to make a proposal. First, despite this representing the reversal of a previous ruling, I propose a thanks to the current draft cover resolution. Of the two paragraphs agreed to yesterday, working group based on the proposal made by the UE, wanting to preamble on the other one, the independent session, as read by the chair of the working group just now. The agreed language is to be taken exactly as this. So we invite delegations to accept it now by consensus. And we hope that this is taken as a sign of good faith and a reminder of what we can achieve working together. Second, we are calling immediately for two independent votes. First, we shall vote to decide on action point one between the report from the drafting group. Partish of we invited to vote only available text options referring to tacit or explicit acceptance. Here, of course, I invite parties not to change IMO practice any more than we already have. By voting on the first option, referring to tacit also as a sign of good faith. The second vote will refer to the adoption of the amendments themselves. Partish shall cast a vote of yes if they wish to adopt it or no if they wish otherwise. We wish to say all parties would be invited to adopt the measures so we can start working on the guidelines. We consider this discussions have been exhausted sir and we believe it's time to check whether multilateralism also has remained confident on the things that we can achieve working together. We've seen them. So Colleagues from the islands let's finally build a study source of revenue for supporting adaptation to climate change and capacity building initiatives in the sector. EU, China, Saudi and others less for innovation. The US, India, let's build on the biofuels global alliance that we have and see how we can help the IMO. Resonance and respect all the positions. We think all positions are legitimate and must be respected as part of our sovereign spaces. And we understand no one means any type of harm or hostility to others just by disagree. And even as we may disagree sometimes we can also work to weave together our priorities and have our cooperation be the very fabric of this new instrument. So let's understand that this is not a zero sum game. And let's do all that while reducing GHG emissions to zero in the sector by around 2050 as we agree. thereby doing what we can in this organization which still matters to send the planet a signal of hope. Thank you. Thank you, Brazil for your intervention and once again for yesterday. I apologize for as you read this data. This is an extra ordinary session. Extra ordinary seven instances. That requires extra ordinary adaptation as we proceed as issues evolve. So my apology. I request for independent votes. I'm sure we'll get to the hour. Our chanting group report that will be taken into consideration. With that I give the full three United States. Thank you very much, Cher. Good morning, everyone. Maybe in the spirit of the great hug between our respective heads of state. I will agree a bit with Brazil this morning, though not in full. I don't think that'll be a surprise. But I do think that multilateralism took a strange turn last night. I appreciate the efforts of many in the room. Interesting conversations were being had about the best path forward conversations that for many I think were earnest. I had we've all been made aware that something happened. But before moving on, we are here to be accountable ultimately to our citizens. And we cannot do diplomacy behind closed doors in this way. I think the conversations that were had were very important. But if we ignore for the permanent record what happened last night, I think that is a disservice to the citizens of our countries. The secretary general and others led conversations that I think were commendable in the spirit of the organization. As it has been discussed by so many over this entire week. The spirit of multilateralism is conversations. The spirit of multilateralism is getting to consensus despite deep, deep divisions. And I think the secretary general was attempting to do that. I heard varying positions from a diverse array of countries. Some that surprised me. Some that did not surprise me. But I think it would be a disservice to the citizens of our countries if it were at least not summarized for the record. The portion of our proceedings that occurred as Brazil rightly pointed out outside of this room yesterday. So I put that to the membership for consideration, for discussion, if the chair or the secretary general wish to make a statement about what happened during the recess last night. That would be most welcomed. But I would imagine some of these others also want to speak on the issue. So thank you. Thank you. You as our makeup comment on that. And as you may have come in about the other cars, I will come into you. Here, please. Thank you chair and good morning everybody. We have seen the proper reports of the working group on reduction of GST, efficient from ships. Notice that Kenya's name is MISIC. We are there in the group, so that can be included. We appreciate that. Thank you chair. Yeah, that would be done. Sorry, I really appreciate it. Thank you very much chair. First, it's nice to see again this full house here in the IMO. It's very apparent that everyone is eager to find out what the next step this committee will take as a membership. What's clear to our delegation is that we're in whether we like it or not or whether we acknowledge or not historic moments here. They're incorrect about that. But we do have an opportunity at the same time to stop the finger pointing, to stop the hostilities between us. And to come together and find the way to move forward without the need to continue sewing divisions between us and show unity, overcoming the differences that we hold. And our differences are not because we have not intent as Brazil had mentioned. And are not because we want it divided. And differences because we are all looking out for our citizens, our futures, developing a VIMO from our perspective, and our economies. So we can choose the needs of division. It will be us for the rules. We're comfortable to do that. Or we can choose the path of unity and move forward together. This is an option. Proceedability is very comfortable with behalf proceedings to play conversations happening in the poor doors. And we agree with the United States that it's important that these conversations are about the best, more and just here and in the next minute. And so that the options are laid bare for the world to see and for delegates to have an opportunity to choose unity or choose division. And we're happy to choose unity. But if others insist on division, we are well prepared and we will not fly away from that. Our explanation we look forward to hear from the Secretary General and to see how these discussions play out. Thank you very much. Thank you, Saudi Arabia. Saudi Arabia, please. Thank you, Chairman. Udha, please. Thank you, Chair. We would like to align ourselves with the comments made by Brazil and support the motion forward. Who ever would. Thank you. Thank you, Chairman. I just want to give the floor to Secretary General to respond to the comments from the U.S. and Saudi Arabia. Yes, please. Thank you, Chair and good morning to all. Sorry about that. I'm losing my voice even though I have not been making many interventions in this plenary. Sorry about how that's happening. I spoke to you, thank you as long as I said that this is not a typical eye-womiting and a stand-by-dance. And I mean that by the way, that all of you, all of you without exception, have been or have not been negotiating with each other. You know that I always speak to you as a whole, the same way that I defend you all. And I do convey for transparency. I wish I would have the opportunity to speak to many of you yesterday, but I did. And yes, the allegations are taken. I'm sorry. Some of you have been operating. I put myself out there to try to find a way for you to cooperate and move forward in the normal way that we work. And for me to make any special, even two, pause for a little more time for you to debate, because this is a member state, led process, I will have to be sure, convinced that all of you would like to take that path with. So there is some why, with apologies, I took some time away from your meeting yesterday. However, I do respect the mixed comments that I received from several of you. I'm not going to talk about sides. I'm just going to say from several of you, which led me to not take any action at this stage. From my side, that I will respect the decision, the process, and the way that you move forward. I can guarantee that you can count on me to be helpful when you consider that I can be to assist you. When you think that I can assist you, and to continue working towards further achievements in every subject in this organization. And I will be, and thank you, to Ron, as well, when we finish this meeting today. Hopefully the way that we, and I go into include myself here, and we have been having me for this week. And this how doesn't repeat again. Again, I wish I would have the opportunity to speak to all of you, but now you or no, was thrown into the way I was coming through. But the decisions are all yours. Thank you, Chair. Thank you, SG. I will take the order cards, and then I see you guys say something. I will take the order cards. Eventually, and then I will give them on a terms of what I think is the work of corridor diplomacy. And it's well, please. As in preparation, please. Thank you, Mr. Chairman. Good morning. Good morning, General Secretary General. Good morning to colleagues. I am glad that you and I are with us, Mr. Chairman, and not somewhere in the hospital. Mr. Chairman, in addition to the fact that colleagues from the United States and colleagues from Saudi Arabia, which we support in the coming days, we would like to express our side, the deepest disappointment I have experienced. And what happened yesterday after the defeat? Mr. Chairman, what I said is now with General Secretary, that this situation is not similar to our organization. It is even a small word. I don't understand where I am. Mr. Chairman, as you know, we have a hybrid meeting. And not everyone present in the room, in our room. I think that not only from our country, but also from other countries, the Ministry of the Ministry of the Ministry of the Ministry of the Ministry, are listening to our speech and our procedures here. So, I, in the first yesterday, cannot answer colleagues from the Ministry of the Ministry of the Ministry, what is happening here? I just don't know, I don't know what to answer. Because yesterday I really went to some house. In the 15th, where I was in the 14th, there was a consultation with one delegation. And nothing happened at all. And you, and all the delegates are sitting in the room. And you see, Mr. Chairman, such presidents can't just be in our organization. I see I'm already in the morning. I want this statement to be taken to the final agreement. Singapore, please. Thank you, Chair. Singapore appreciates the Secretary General's update and the efforts by all delegations in working towards consensus on the IMO-NAT-0 framework and shares the concerns that he has cited. We recognise the significant progress that we have achieved last year. However, Chair, discussions this past week have indicated that more time may be needed for member states and industry to understand how best to move the framework forward. In this regard, we shared a view expressed earlier that this is indeed an extraordinary meeting and we are at an important crossroad. Choosing between unity or division. Singapore chooses unity. We are concerned that proceeding to a vote at this juncture without sufficient clarity or comfort across delegations could risk fragmenting the spirit of cooperation that has characterized the WTO, this process and process is... All member states, our citizens and our economies and would not provide certainty and clarity for our industry and companies. In view of this, Singapore would like to propose that the vote on the adoption of the draft revised MAPO NX-6 be deferred to a later session to allow additional time for delegations to consolidate their national positions and further engagement to be conducted. This approach will help ensure that the eventual decision reflects the common will among member states. Singapore urges all member states to support this proposal for deferment in the shared interest of a cohesive and durable outcome for the IMO NX-0 framework. Thank you, Chair.

Singapore appreciates the Secretary General's update and the efforts by all delegations in working towards consensus on the IMO-NAT-0 framework and shares the concerns that he has cited.
